message:
fix(extras,render): add NaN guards before f64::clamp in reveal progress and diff quantile

Two independent but thematically identical fixes that harden floating-point
sanitization against NaN inputs:

1. `text_effects.rs` — `RevealMode::is_visible()`: The `progress` parameter
   is clamped to [0.0, 1.0] before use, but `f64::clamp()` propagates NaN
   per IEEE 754 semantics (NaN compared to anything is false, so clamp
   returns NaN unchanged). If a NaN progress value slipped through, it
   would bypass both the `>= 1.0` and `<= 0.0` early-return guards
   (since NaN comparisons are always false), then propagate into the
   per-mode arithmetic, causing incorrect visibility results. The fix
   maps NaN to 0.0 (fully hidden) before clamping, which is the safest
   default — better to show nothing than to show garbage.

2. `diff_strategy.rs` — `DiffStrategyConfig::sanitized()`: The
   `conservative_quantile` field is clamped to [EPS, 1-EPS], but the
   same NaN-propagation issue applies. A NaN quantile would flow into
   the Bayesian threshold computation and silently corrupt budget
   decisions. The fix maps NaN to EPS (the most conservative setting),
   which matches the defensive spirit of the sanitization function —
   when in doubt, assume the worst case and over-scan rather than
   under-scan.

Both fixes follow the same pattern: check `is_nan()` first, substitute a
safe fallback, then clamp. This is consistent with how the neighboring
`normalize_positive` and `normalize_cost` helpers already handle
non-finite values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
